description: Global HIV/AIDS pandemic reaching epidemic scale; deeply impacted music and culture especially in Europe and the Americas
Largest single-day percentage stock market crash in history on 19 October 1987; short-lived but globally felt
US savings and loan association crisis involving widespread insolvency of thrift institutions; cost ~$160bn
Global recession following the Gulf War oil price shock and tight monetary policy in the US and UK
US-led coalition conflict against Iraq following its invasion of Kuwait; 34-nation coalition deployed
Series of ethnic wars following dissolution of Yugoslavia; included Bosnian War and Croatian War of Independence
Mass slaughter of Tutsi and moderate Hutu populations; approximately 800,000 killed in 100 days
Currency and financial crisis beginning in Thailand spreading rapidly across Southeast and East Asia
Armed conflict in Kosovo between the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia and the Kosovo Liberation Army
Collapse of overvalued internet company stocks following the speculative bubble of the late 1990s
September 11 attacks and subsequent US-led military campaigns in Afghanistan and the broader War on Terror
Severe acute respiratory syndrome epidemic originating in Guangdong; first novel coronavirus outbreak
US-led invasion of Iraq and subsequent decade-long occupation; over 200,000 civilian casualties estimated
Worst global financial crisis since the Great Depression triggered by US subprime mortgage collapse
Influenza pandemic originating in Mexico; WHO declared first pandemic of the 21st century in June 2009
Wave of pro-democracy protests and armed uprisings across the Arab world; toppled governments in Tunisia Egypt Libya
Sovereign debt crisis affecting Greece Portugal Ireland Spain and Cyprus threatening Eurozone stability
Multifaceted civil conflict in Syria; over 600,000 deaths and 13 million displaced creating Europe's largest refugee crisis
Largest Ebola outbreak in history centred in Guinea Liberia and Sierra Leone; over 11,000 deaths
Russian military annexation of Crimea from Ukraine in February–March 2014; marked major geopolitical rupture
Global respiratory pandemic caused by SARS-CoV-2; WHO declared public health emergency January 2020
Sharpest global economic contraction since records began; GDP fell 3.1% globally in 2020
Full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine launched February 2022; largest European land war since WWII
Global surge in consumer price inflation following pandemic supply shocks compounded by Ukraine war energy crisis
